new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PDO, actual alcoholic strength > 15% vol, in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2204 21 74)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein aus frischen Weintrauben mit geschützter Ursprungsbezeichnung (g.U.), tatsächlicher Alkoholgehalt > 15 % vol, in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2204 21 74)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected designation of origin (PDO) as defined in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, with an actual alcoholic strength by volume exceeding 15% vol, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres. Corresponds to CN code 2204 21 74.

coverage_explanation: The legal text identifies CN 2204 21 74 as a PDO still wine in containers ≤ 2 L (covered by SmallContainer restriction and ProtectedDesignationOfOriginQuality restriction, inherited from StillWineWithPDO2204Small parent). The alcoholic strength threshold (> 15% vol) is captured via the decimalRange restriction on alcoholicStrengthByVolumePercent. However, the specific content of Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 (which defines what qualifies as a PDO wine within the 2204 21 11–78 range) is not fully modelled — the note itself defines PDO criteria not yet formalised as OWL axioms. Additionally, the sibling sub-range context (2204 21 11–78) and the precise upper bound of the alcoholic-strength bracket for 22042174 (which from CN structure is > 15% vol with no stated upper cap within PDO wines in this size class) cannot be fully expressed without more legal note details. Coverage is partial due to these gaps.
